title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options · Behavior Case: Pacing Curve
summary: Score drifts from destruction records, waves advance before fragments resolve, the ship respawns inside danger, pacing accelerates without respite, and assistance modes shame the player. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pacing curve increment in fair progression and difficulty model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Shape action and recovery intervals using bounded curves, playtest cohorts, and separate parameter evidence.
Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-pacing-curve — Shape action and recovery intervals using bounded curves, playtest cohorts, and separate parameter evidence.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-score-progression-spawning-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-score-progression-spawning-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_score_progression_spawning_5(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-pacing-curve-7xa4tx
# Competency: asteroids-pacing-curve.
# Case 5555: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_pacing_curve_7xa4tx(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_7xa4tx = "7xa4tx-xt4ax7-5555"
    assert evidence_variant_7xa4tx
    evidence_axes_7xa4tx = (
        "player7xa4tx",
        "seed5555",
        "inputxt4ax7",
        "frame7xa4tx5555",
        "state55557xa4tx",
        "playtestxt4ax75555",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_7xa4tx)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"

options.text: For repair the pacing curve defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Pacing Curve for fair progression and difficulty model.
Reconstruct the conditions for Pacing Curve, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Pacing Curve artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the pacing curve defect evidence boundary for Pacing Curve.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Pacing Curve model · Theory Pacing Curve
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Pacing Curve: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 7824 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Shape action and recovery intervals using bounded curves, playtest cohorts, and separate parameter evidence.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Pacing Curve model · Theory Pacing Curve.
steps.why: Retrieving Pacing Curve through the concrete fair progression and difficulty model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Pacing Curve model · Theory Pacing Curve, keep Pacing Curve connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Shape action and recovery intervals using bounded curves, playtest cohorts, and separate parameter evidence.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Pacing Curve model · Theory Pacing Curve, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Pacing Curve precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Pacing Curve model · Theory Pacing Curve, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 7824 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Shape action and recovery intervals using bounded curves, playtest cohorts, and separate parameter evidence.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Pacing Curve model · Theory Pacing Curve, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Difficulty should increase linearly forever to remain challenging.
